1.11a: Contact the Completions Team at the office issuing mortgage instructions.
Specific contact details are provided in the Mortgage Offer.
1.11b: Contact the Completions Team at the office issuing instructions with recommendations for document changes.
Contact details are in the Mortgage Offer.
1.7: Contact the Completions Team at the office issuing instructions for cases where borrower and mortgagor differ.
Refer to the Mortgage Offer for contact details.
1.14: Firm may not act if the person handling the case or their immediate family is the seller.
Firm may act if a separate fee earner of equal standing or a partner acts for the lender.
1.15: Firm may not act if the person handling the case or their immediate family is the borrower.
Firm may act if a separate fee earner of equal standing or a partner acts for the lender.
3.1.3: Notification of seller's solicitor details is required only upon specific request.
3.1.4: Notify the Completions Team at the office issuing instructions if the seller has no legal representation.
See Mortgage Offer for contact details.
3.1.5: Contact the Completions Team at the office issuing instructions for identity verification document requirements.
3.2.3: Notification of seller's solicitor details is required only upon specific request.
3.2.4: Notify the Completions Team at the office issuing instructions if the seller has no legal representation.
4.5a: Contact the Completions Team at the office issuing instructions if a re-inspection is required.
4.5b: Send Certificate of Title discharge to the Customer Service Centre.
Address and fax details are provided on the redemption statement.
4.1: The lender may opt not to conduct a physical inspection.
Valuation reports are electronic and cannot be forwarded.
Solicitors must obtain a copy of the valuation report directly from the customer.
4.3: Notify the Completions Team at the office issuing instructions if valuation assumptions are incorrect.
5.1.1: Remortgage applications require the borrower to have owned the property for at least 6 months.
Sub-sales, back-to-back transactions, and assignments involving intervening sellers are not acceptable.
Seller acquiring title and immediately granting a lease to the borrower is considered an unacceptable sub-sale.
Assignable, fulfilment, assisted, or novation contracts are prohibited.
Irrevocable powers of attorney in favor of intervening sellers are prohibited.
Report any Unilateral Notice registered against the freehold title relating to the security address.
5.2.1: Assignable contracts or irrevocable powers of attorney for intervening sellers are unacceptable.
Report any similar transaction structures to the Completions Team.
5.4.4: Do not send environmental or contaminated land reports to the lender.
5.4.5: Search firms should be members of a trade body, have adequate insurance, and abide by the Property Ombudsman scheme.
5.5.3a: Notify the Completions Team if a breach of planning or building regulations is within the enforcement period.
Notification is not required if the enforcement period has expired.
5.5.3b: Lender does not require planning permission documents; forward to borrower.
5.5.3c: Lender does not require building regulation consents; forward to borrower.
5.5.3d: Lender does not require certificates of lawful use or development; forward to borrower.
5.5.4: Report restrictions affecting value or marketability to the Completions Team.
See Mortgage Offer for contact details.
5.7.1a: Flying or creeping freeholds are accepted if they comprise only part of the title.
5.7.1c: Report accepted flying freeholds or freehold flats to the Completions Team.
5.8.1: Buildings converted into max 4 flats are accepted if borrower occupies one and owns/jointly owns the freehold.
Accepted for Buy-to-Let with no borrower occupation requirement.
Report title arrangements if not mentioned in the valuation report.
5.8.5: Security involving two leasehold flats where owners cross-hold freehold reversions is accepted.
The lease must mandate simultaneous transfer of the freehold share upon sale of the lease.
Requires a mortgage on the borrower's leasehold flat and the borrower's freehold interest in the other flat.
5.9.1: Commonhold tenure is not accepted for new mortgages.
Existing mortgages are considered on a case-by-case basis.
5.10.1: Advise the Completions Team of any use restrictions and their impact on security.
5.13.1: Report if the balance of purchase price is not from the borrower's own funds or involves a second charge to ConveyancerSupportTeam@lloydsbanking.com.
Gifted deposits accepted only from immediate family (birth, blood, spouse, civil partner, child, co-habitee).
Gifts from unrelated third parties, friends, or employers are not accepted.
Deeds of Trust or Second Charges to protect gifts are not acceptable.
Family gifted deposits are prohibited in Buy-to-Let where the vendor is a family member.
Gift letter must be dated within 3 months of completion and confirm gift is non-repayable and no interest is acquired.
Bankruptcy searches required for all parties contributing to the purchase price.
5.14.15a: Contact the Completions Team at the office issuing instructions regarding absentee/insolvent landlords.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absent or insolvent landlords is accepted subject to section 9 requirements.
5.14.17a: Send executed Leaseholder Certificate, confirmation of submission to landlord, and Landlord Certificate after completion.
Remortgages are exempt from this requirement.
5.14.17b: Report non-qualifying leaseholders or remediation cost liabilities before completion.
Written instructions from the lender are required before proceeding to completion.
5.14.17c: Requirements in 5.14.17a and 5.14.17b do not apply to remortgages.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term must be 70 years from the date of the mortgage.
5.14.9: Contact the Completions Team at the office issuing instructions for lease matters.
5.14.10: Contact the Completions Team at the office issuing instructions for service charge matters.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms is accepted subject to section 9 requirements.
5.14.12: Lender does not require ground rent or service charge receipts; forward to borrower.
5.14.13: Supply a copy of the notice of service on completion; original to borrower.
5.15.2a: Contact the Completions Team regarding management company issues.
5.15.2b: Lender does not require management company share certificates; forward to borrower.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms are not required.
5.15.2d: Supply copies of memorandum and articles of association on completion; originals to borrower.
5.16.2: Contact the Completions Team if unable to certify that a search entry does not relate to the property.
5.17.5a: Lender does not require the Power of Attorney document.
5.17.5b: Statutory declaration of non-revocation of Power of Attorney is not required.
5.19.1: Affordable housing, shared equity, and shared ownership schemes are not supported.
5.20.1: Report solar PV panel leases that do not meet UK Finance minimum requirements to the section 1.11a contact.
5.20.4: Disclosure of existing Green Deal Plans is not required.
6.1.3: Notify the Completions Team if the borrower does not take up the mortgage offer.
6.2.1: Contact the Completions Team regarding discrepancies in property description.
6.3.1: Distressed sales (avoiding repossession or sale & leaseback with AST) are not acceptable.
Contact the Completions Team for purchase price issues.
6.4.4: Report if cash incentives are 10% higher than stated in the offer or if no details are in the offer.
Report if total cash/financial incentives exceed 5% of the purchase price.
Report non-financial incentives like cars, excluding fixed household fittings (kitchens, carpets, etc.).
6.4.5: Contact the Completions Team if the solicitor will not have control over the payment of all purchase money.
6.5.1: Contact the Completions Team if vacant possession is not being provided.
6.6.1: Distressed sales involving sale & leaseback under an AST are prohibited.
6.6.2: Contact the Completions Team if letting details are unavailable at completion.
6.6.3: Lender does not require tenancy agreement copies; forward to borrower.
6.6.4: HMOs are accepted with a maximum of 5 unrelated occupiers.
Requires a Single Assured Shorthold Tenancy and a valid mandatory HMO licence.
6.7.1: Acceptable warranty schemes: NHBC, Premier Guarantee, BLP, Buildzone, Checkmate, LABC, Protek, ICW, etc.
Building standards indemnity cover required only for first-time occupancy.
6.7.2: Lender does not require warranty documentation; forward to borrower.
6.7.3: Lender does not require warranty assignments; forward to borrower.
6.7.4: Lender will proceed without a warranty scheme if there are max 15 properties on site and construction was monitored by a professional consultant.
6.7.6: Lender does not require the professional consultant's certificate; forward to borrower.
6.8.1: Notification/retention not required for unadopted roads/sewers managed by residents/management companies or if bond/agreement not granted prior to completion.
6.9.1: Contact the Completions Team if necessary easements are absent.
6.10.2: Retentions or mortgage advance instalments will be released to the borrower.
6.11.1: Contact the Completions Team if the property is affected by redevelopment or road proposals.
6.12.1: Contact the Completions Team if pre-emption rights, resale restrictions, or options affect security.
6.13.1: Contact the Completions Team if the property is affected by an undischarged improvement/repair grant.
7.3: Consent to mortgage required for all occupants aged 17 or over, excluding borrower's or co-habitee's children.
7.4: Contact the Completions Team regarding accuracy of disclosed information.
8.1: Solicitors may advise specified third parties if there is no risk of the transaction being challenged.
9.1: Lender does not require the indemnity insurance policy; forward to borrower.
9.2: Minimum indemnity limit must be 110% of the purchase price or property value, whichever is higher.
10.2a: Advances are paid electronically via CHAPS with no additional fee.
10.2b: Standard notice requirement is 3 full working days.
Lender will attempt to meet urgent requests if 3 days' notice is not possible.
10.3: Standard deductions include retentions or sums specified in the Mortgage Offer.
10.7: Delayed completion funds must be returned electronically within 1 working day.
Do not deduct charges from returned funds.
Account for prefix '20': Lloyds Bank, Sort 30-02-50, Acc 04968773.
Use codes: RAD (Returned advance) or FADV (Returned further advance).
10.9: Contact the Completions Team if completion is delayed.
10.10: Mortgage advance can be held for a maximum of one working day.
10.11: Interest on delayed returns is charged at the contractual rate from the date funds were sent.
12.3.1: Contact the Post Completions Team at the office issuing instructions for release of retentions.
14.1.4: Form CH2 application is not required.
14.1.5: Lender does not require original mortgage deeds or title documents; solicitor must retain copies.
14.2.1: Deeds address: 1 Lovell Park Road, Leeds, LS1 1NS. DX 11896 LEEDS LOVELL PARK.
14.2.2: Post-completion documents: Official Copies showing first charge, deeds of guarantee, indemnity schedules, Power of Attorney copies, consent forms, and Leaseholder/Landlord certificates.
Do not send pre-registration deeds or lease agreements.
16.1.1: Title deeds address for completions pre-May 2000: 1 Lovell Park Road, Leeds, LS1 1NS.
No deeds are held for completions post-May 2000.
16.3.7a: Contact the Transfers Section at Birmingham Midshires Mortgage Servicing, PO Box 833, Leeds for transfer execution.
16.3.7b: Standard attestation: 'Executed as a Deed by as attorney for and on behalf of Bank of Scotland plc'.
Effective date of transfer must be confirmed to the lender.
16.3.1: Standard transfer/deed of covenant forms are provided with instructions.
Custom forms are permitted if they safeguard lender interests.
16.3.2: Debt amount contact: Loans Servicing, Birmingham Midshires Mortgage Servicing, PO Box 833, Leeds, LS1 9PU.
16.3.4: Send transfer of equity only if lender execution is required.
16.4.2: Lender does not need to see proposed tenancy agreements for Buy-to-Let products.

16.5.3b: Send Deed of Rectification to Deeds and Sealing Team, PO Box 833, Leeds, LS1 9PU.
